results.name: System MBR-to-GPT conversion in disposable boot VM

evidence.vm_id: SAK-PM-BIOS-MBR-Fixture-20260603-091138 converted disk; pre-conversion snapshot pre-system-mbr2gpt-20260603-1110 UUID=f5ea0620-64d3-4ced-826c-811091364e10; fresh EFI boot VM SAK-PM-MBR2GPT-EFI-Boot-20260603-1140
evidence.boot_result: Passed: mbr2gpt converted the disposable boot/system disk from MBR to GPT and created an EFI System partition; after VirtualBox UEFI boot-file normalization with bcdboot/fallback BOOTX64.EFI, the same converted disk booted in fresh EFI VM SAK-PM-MBR2GPT-EFI-Boot-20260603-1140 to GuestAdditionsRunLevel=3, VideoMode=1024,768,32; poll_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/system-mbr2gpt/fresh-efi-boot-20260603-1140/fresh-efi-boot-poll.json

results.name: OS migration target boot and firmware-order proof

evidence.clone_verification_mode: VBoxManage clonevm copied powered-off source VM, then target VM was stripped to cloned SATA-0-0 OS disk only before boot verification.
evidence.firmware_boot_order: Target VM firmware=EFI, SecureBoot=off, boot1=disk; non-OS cloned SATA/NVMe/USB media detached before boot; target-showvminfo artifacts capture full order.
evidence.target_boot_result: Passed: disposable target-only cloned OS VM reached Guest Additions run level 3 and produced a valid 1024x768 screenshot after boot.

results.name: UEFI boot repair with intentionally broken BCD

evidence.vm_id: SAK-PM-OS-Migration-ConfigClone-20260603-073901; pre-repair snapshot pre-uefi-bcd-break-20260603-0833 UUID=8f9871ac-77bb-46d2-af82-87e9e96be10a; offline repair runner SAK-PM-LAB
evidence.broken_boot_symptom: BCD and fallback loader removed on disposable target disk 3; target boot screenshot shows BdsDxe failed to load Windows Boot Manager and no bootable option; GuestAdditionsRunLevel stayed 0 for six polls; poll_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/boot-repair-uefi/broken-20260603-083630/broken-boot-poll.json
evidence.repair_commands: Disk GUID reset to 969b2a76-8cfa-464f-9834-c22abacb396a; bcdboot D:\Windows /s S: /f UEFI exited 0; fallback BOOTX64.EFI exists=True; commands_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/offline-uefi-repair/20260603-085333/offline-uefi-repair-commands.json
evidence.post_repair_boot_result: Passed: target VM reached VMState=running, GuestAdditionsRunLevel=3, VideoMode=1024,768,32"@0,0 1 after repair; poll_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/boot-repair-uefi/post-repair-20260603-085333/post-repair-boot-poll.json

results.name: BIOS/MBR boot repair with intentionally broken BCD

evidence.vm_id: SAK-PM-BIOS-MBR-Fixture-20260603-091138; pre-repair snapshot pre-bios-bcd-break-20260603-1036 UUID=bb3b4b6c-946a-422f-9517-c1d0c4e05319; offline repair runner SAK-PM-LAB
evidence.broken_boot_symptom: BCD removed from disposable MBR target W:\Boot\BCD; target boot reached Windows Recovery screen: File: \\Boot\\BCD, Error code: 0xc000000f; GuestAdditionsRunLevel stayed 0 for eight polls; poll_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/boot-repair-bios/broken-20260603-104917/broken-boot-poll.json
evidence.repair_commands: bcdboot W:\Windows /s W: /f BIOS exited 0; bootsect /nt60 W: /mbr /force exited 0; bcdedit /store W:\Boot\BCD /enum all exited 0; commands_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/offline-bios-repair/20260603-110420/offline-bios-repair-commands.json
evidence.post_repair_boot_result: Passed: repaired target VM reached VMState=running, GuestAdditionsRunLevel=3, VideoMode=1024,768,32 after repair; poll_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/boot-repair-bios/post-repair-20260603-110420/post-repair-boot-poll.json

results.name: USB removable disk destructive operation proof

evidence.operation: Created FAT32 fixture volume, wrote hash-verified files, then cleared disposable USB disk.

results.name: File-level Data Recovery scan and restore proof

evidence.scan_result: Recovered matching deleted PDF fixture recovered_0000007d3000.pdf from raw source \\.\D: at offset 8204288 after 1 candidate(s).

results.name: SSD/NVMe secure erase command execution proof

evidence.media_type: VirtualBox SATA nonrotational SSD fixture; VirtualBox SATA port 2 configured nonrotational=on; host proof artifact artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/vbox-showvminfo-ssd-usb.txt; FriendlyName=VBOX HARDDISK; SerialOrUniqueId=VB5603ba00-a26a1341     ATAVBOX HARDDISK
evidence.purge_command: Optimize-Volume -ReTrim then Clear-Disk -RemoveData -RemoveOEM on disposable VirtualBox NVMe disk; ReTrim exit_code=0.
evidence.post_purge_identity_check: Disk 2 still reports VBOX HARDDISK serial/unique 'VB5603ba00-a26a1341' and partition style RAW with 0 partitions after purge.

evidence.locked_state_blocker: This drive is locked by BitLocker Drive Encryption. You must unlock this drive from Control Panel.
evidence.operation_attempted: Write attempt while locked, then write/hash after recovery-password unlock
